title: Global regularity for a logarithmically supercritical hyperdissipative Navier-Stokes equation

thm:1.1.summary: Global smooth solutions for generalized NSE with dissipation $m(\xi) \ge |\xi|^{(d+2)/4}/g(|\xi|)$ when $\int$₁$^\infty$ ds/(s $g(s)^4) = +\infty$. Covers logarithmically supercritical case $m(\xi) = |\xi|^{(d+2)/4}/log^{1/4}(2+|\xi|^2)$. Proof: higher-order Sobolev energy $E_k$ satisfies $\partial_t E_k \le$ C $g(1+E_k)^4 E_k(1+a(t))$ after Littlewood-Paley frequency splitting at N $= 1+E_k$. Divergence condition on g prevents ODE blowup via separation of variables.

rem:1.2.summary: Heuristic argument that the exponent 4 in $\intds/sg^4=\infty$ should be improvable to 2 (i.e. $\intds/sg^2=\infty)$. Blowup scenario: energy concentrates on ball of radius 1/N(t), gives blowup rate N(t) ~ (T*-t)^{-2/(d+2)}. Energy inequality then contradicts blowup if $\intds/sg^2=\infty$. Author unable to make rigorous — different definitions of frequency scale N(t) lose powers of g(s). Going beyond $g^2-barrier$ would require forcing turbulent energy distribution.

rem:2.1.summary: The proof extends to torus $R^d/Z^d$ and other smooth domains by replacing Littlewood-Paley operators $P_{\leN}, P_{>N}$ with spectral projections of the Laplacian.

edges.note: Analyzes whether thm:1.1's divergence condition $(\intds/sg^4=\infty)$ can be weakened to $\intds/sg^2=\infty$. Uses the same energy framework but with a blowup-rate argument instead of direct energy estimates.
Notes that the Littlewood-Paley decomposition in thm:1.1's proof can be replaced by spectral projections on other domains (torus, smooth bounded domains).

layer_1_main_theorem.description: Single monolithic proof via energy method + Littlewood-Paley decomposition + ODE comparison

layer_2_commentary.description: Heuristic improvements $(g^4\tog^2$ barrier) and domain generalizations
